calendar.matters: PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY ERIC MATHISEN

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ALRIGHT SOLUTIONS, LLC AN ILLINOIS LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, REED GROUP, LLC MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Alright Solutions, LLC and Reed Group, LLC's unopposed Motion to Admit Pro Hac Vice Counsel Eric P. Mathisen is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, counsel Eric P. Mathisen may appear as counsel pro hac vice for Moving Parties in this action.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Alight Solutions, LLC and Reed Group, LLC's unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Eric P. Mathisen Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Eric P. Mathisen has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) Counsel declares he "ha[s] not applied for appearance or appeared pro hac vice in any other matters in the state courts of California in the last two years." Counsel's testimony begs the question whether he has applied to appear PHV in any other court, including any federal court, in this state? The clear intent of the requirement is to assure the court counsel is not seeking to appear PHV with such frequency that they should seek admission to the State Bar rather than PHV status. On this record the court cannot make the necessary determination.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
